name: Salmon Coulibiac
recipeIngredient: 1 1.5 lb Poached salmon, boned and flaked, chilled
recipeInstructions:
Salmon coulibiac is a traditional Yule holiday dish in Russia because it looks like a present: red (salmon) and green (spinach), all gift-wrapped in puff pastry. This dish would also be ideal on a New Year's Eve or Twelfth Night buffet.
1. Place 2 (15x12-inch) puff pastry sheets side by side on a floured board.
2. For each puff pastry, spoon 1/4 cup Rice Filling evenly over the center of each pastry.
3. Add Mushroom-Spinach Filling evenly on top of rice mixture.
4. Distribute flaked salmon evenly over mushroom mixture.
5. Top each with remaining Rice Filling.
6. Beat egg with half and half in a small bowl to make egg wash.
7. Brush edges of pastry with egg wash.
8. Fold pastry lengthwise, overlapping edges.
9. Tuck ends to seal.
10. Turn over, seam-side down onto a greased baking sheet.
11. Make cutouts from remaining (10x4-inch) puff pastry sheets using pastry cutters.
12. Place on top and sides of pastry roll.
13. Make vent in center of roll and brush entire surface and cutouts with egg mixture.
14. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes, or until golden brown.
15. Remove from oven and allow to cool 10 minutes before slicing.
16. Serve hot or at room temperature with Cucumber-Dill Sauce. Makes about 10 servings.
Rice Filling
2 tbsp butter
1/2 cup chopped shallots
2 cloves garlic, minced
3 cups cooked rice
1 bunch fresh dill, chopped
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add shallots and garlic. Saute until shallots are tender. Add rice and dill and mix well.
Mushroom-Spinach Filling
1/4 cup butter
1 onion, chopped
1/2 pound mushrooms, sliced 1/4-inch thick
1 (10-ounce) package frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
Salt and pepper
Melt butter in a small skillet. Add onion and saute until tender, about 1 minute. Add mushrooms and spinach and cook 3 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Cucumber-Dill Sauce
2 tbsp butter
1/4 cup minced shallots
1 1/2 tsp flour
1 cup half and half
1 cup sour cream
1/2 cup chopped fresh dill
1/4 cup grated peeled cucumber
Melt butter in a small skillet over medium heat. Add shallots and saute until translucent. Stir in flour and cook 1 minute. Stir in half and half and simmer until slightly thickened. Add sour cream and dill. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Heat through, but do not boil. Add cucumber just before serving. Makes 2 1/2 cups.
